Good morning!
I was wondering when we can get an updated schedule for Friday? Maria Moron Quintero
Good morning! Sorry, I wasn’t aware of the changes for Friday. I’ll send the updated schedule in a moment. Thanks!
Cavanna belt is flaking. maintenance is working on it.

The weight of the final piece went down the operator is making adjustments

Continuing with the buffer emptying test, the 3rd shift lead informed me that the line was started at 5:50 a.m. with the buffer tank at 98%. The line stopped at 8:36 a.m. with the buffer at 28% while running at 1.75 speed / 1,224 pieces per minute.
Approximately 70% of the buffer was used in 166 minutes, averaging about 25% per hour.
The buffer is currently at 32% after pending trim remelt chocolate was added back into the buffer tank once the line stopped.
We repaired 8 holes
Can do
Candon Watt, have you had a chance to look into this?
Rafael Baretta, have you had a chance to look into this?

Can yall walk the perimeter of this everyday to ensure we aren’t seeing anything like this?
Yes we'll get them fixed
Yes, the FG-30521 Rev 01 calls for 40649-V4. It’s uploaded to SC
Please make sure to check and replace the container when it is full. This is in Tunnel 3.
44 pieces
The operator and the packing staff were informed that a small wrinkle is appearing on the jar labels; please keep an eye out for this and take steps to correct it. Thank you.
The labels on the jars were coming out wrinkled, and the date stamp was also shifting to one side. Maintenance made adjustments to the wrap conveyor, and it is now working better.

We were seeing a slight wrinkle on the jar. I noticed that the jars weren't going into the wrapping pad straight, so I adjusted the pad and the wrapping belt to the south by a couple of millimeters. I also noticed the wrinkles were near the top, so I raised the wrapping belt up so it was closer to the middle of the jar. Seems to have helped. No more wrinkles.
Victor Romero Sr. this are not the most updated CL, can you please look into it, and print the right ones

An email will be sent to Ethan regarding the FW V6 dimensions, as it is larger than the size we normally use on the Cavanna. Additionally, the FW thickness is 0.04 mm greater than our standard we use.

Swapped out a bent/jammed piston and the assembly is now ready to be installed.
The Mixer Auger was found with hardened chocolate; the TemTek Mixer Auger unit was checked and found to be switched off.

Imperial was received with the hopper area uninstalled; however, upon checking the HMI screen, I noticed that the hopper temperature setting was On . The SSOP specifies that when the hopper is uninstalled, the hopper temperature must be turned off to prevent the water pump from burning out.
Thank you SO much for catching this and calling it out.

Grey Boscan,

Let’s create a list on the board on all the things we are finding and Raoul Lubain can review them tonight with the teams.

Grey Boscan,

Let’s create a list on the board on all the things we are finding and Raoul Lubain can review them tonight with the teams.

As we discussed in the meeting today.  It is better to run at the higher end of the temper when running rice to help reduce clogging.  I know it is difficult with the challenges we have had in controlling temper. 
 
Rich BrierleyGrey Boscan, 
 
We want to put 2 totes of rice crisp in the warm room to warm up through your shift today and put one on the line on 2nd and the other on the line on 3rd.  We want to see what each one looks like when it comes to temperature.

There is a leak in the west side of the dishwasher area, it looks like it’s coming from the roof.  Maintenance is looking into leak right now.

It is condensation from dishwasher exhaust 
The exhaust pipe from dishwasher is next to hot air exhaust from boiler room because this morning is cooler than usual but our boiler room is still really hot that created condensation internally 




We are looking into solutions to prevent condensation.

Chad Stone, Curt Willis, Justin Garner, Matt Hatch, Daniel Bringas, Ira Robison, and Marcos Jimenez
 
The current plan for the S4 line is to commission using Classic Caramel first, followed by a 4-week Bequet ramp-up.
 
However, I am receiving conflicting information between the production schedule and Sales regarding the needed Bequet production volume. We also currently only have one pallet of twist wrap film requested for commissioning. Additionally, Ira mentioned the customer may not want that much sellable product due to shelf life concerns.
 
Ira is meeting with Bequet today, and afterward I will schedule a meeting so we can align on the commissioning and ramp-up plan.

Jay Alba, I just forwarded you a quote from Chocotech who is requesting a PO ASAP since Germany has a holiday tomorrow and they will be closed for the rest of the week. They need the PO so Volker can schedule travel and be onsite next week to support the S4 commissioning/startup.
 
Could you please help create the PO by 12 PM today? 
 
Thanks and apologies for the short notice.
Justin Garner who’s the supervisor inspection the new line.
Erandy is running that project
Charity Young we are working on the details you mentioned, but we are noticing some stains on the catch trays are not water stains, is more like related to the material appearance.
We have also removed the dirty roller and we are cleaning it right now

Washed and sanitized buckets. Start fresh with cleaning supplies.

Erandy Garcilazo Ceja tomorrow coordinate the cleaning of the parts in washroom.  Cover during transportation, including a covering the cart as they are being transported to washroom.  Please do not use sprayer as previously discussed.

Please do not let anyone from your shifts move or grave this carts, pallets and equipment. This is part of the environmental clean on Theegarten, we should be finishing tomorrow
Thank you.
